faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about Nolto for the public sector
faq.q1: How do you handle data protection?
faq.a1: Nolto stores all data in the EU, uses no third-party trackers and all users can export or delete their data at any time. We comply with applicable data protection legislation and have agreements with all sub-processors.
faq.q2: How is Nolto different from LinkedIn?
faq.a2: Nolto is built specifically for the public sector with Swedish hosting and no ads or data selling. Unlike LinkedIn, there are no algorithms manipulating your feed and you retain full control of your data.
faq.a3: Yes! Nolto is open source under the MIT license. You can run your own instance within your organization or use our managed service. All instances can collaborate with each other.
faq.a4: All data is stored encrypted in the EU. Users have full control of their data with the ability to export and delete. We never sell data and use no tracking cookies.
faq.q5: Does it work with other organizations?
faq.a5: Absolutely. Nolto is built on open standards that enable collaboration across organizational boundaries. Municipalities, regions and government agencies can easily connect their instances.
faq.a6: Nolto is free to use as open source. We also offer managed services with support and SLA for organizations that want a turnkey solution. Contact us for a quote.

privacy.essentialCookiesDesc: These cookies are strictly necessary for the platform to function and cannot be disabled.
privacy.authCookies: Authentication cookies: Maintain your login session and keep your account secure.
privacy.securityCookies: Security cookies (Cloudflare): Protect against bots and malicious traffic.
privacy.typographyFontsDesc: By default, we load fonts from Google Fonts to provide a consistent visual experience. When fonts are loaded, your IP address is transmitted to Google's servers.
privacy.googleFontsEnabled: Fonts are loaded from Google (your IP is shared with Google)
privacy.preferenceStored: Your preference is stored locally on your device and is not transmitted to our servers.
privacy.noDataBrokers: Third-party data brokers or ad networks
privacy.platformProviderDesc: Nolto is developed using Lovable, a vibe coding tool registered in Sweden (EU).
privacy.dataCenterDesc: Your data is stored and processed in the European Union:
privacy.edgeFunctionsLocation: Edge Functions: EU-preferred routing via Deno Deploy
privacy.cdnLocation: Content Delivery: Cloudflare global network with EU nodes
privacy.subProcessorsDesc: The following service providers process data on behalf of Nolto:
privacy.subProcessorSupabase: Supabase (Database & Auth) - Frankfurt, Germany - EU-US DPF + SCCs
privacy.subProcessorDeno: Deno Deploy (Edge Functions) - Global/EU - EU-US DPF
privacy.subProcessorCloudflare: Cloudflare (CDN & Security) - Global - EU-US DPF + SCCs
privacy.subProcessorCompliance: All sub-processors are certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework and/or have signed Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for GDPR compliance.
privacy.primaryStorage: Primary data storage: European Union (Frankfurt, Germany)
privacy.transferProtection: When data is processed by US-based infrastructure providers, transfers are protected by:
privacy.technicalMeasures: Supplementary technical measures (encryption in transit and at rest)
privacy.federationTransfers: Data transfers to other connected instances may involve servers in various countries. Each remote instance is responsible for its own data protection practices.

cookies.title: Cookie Notice
cookies.description: We use only essential cookies for authentication and security. We do not use tracking cookies or share data with third parties. Fonts are self-hosted for privacy.
cookies.essentialDesc: Required for authentication, security (Cloudflare), and basic functionality.

mfa.title: Two-Factor Authentication
mfa.description: Add an extra layer of security by requiring a verification code when you sign in.
mfa.enabledDesc: Your account is protected with two-factor authentication.
mfa.federatedNote: As a connected user, your security settings are managed by your home instance. Configure 2FA on your server to protect your account.
mfa.enrollDesc: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app.
mfa.enrollDescMobile: Add your account to an authenticator app.
mfa.enrollSuccessDesc: Your account is now protected with 2FA.
mfa.openAuthenticatorHint: This will add Nolto to your authenticator app automatically
mfa.cantScan: Can't scan? Enter this code manually:
mfa.enterCode: Enter the 6-digit code from your app:
mfa.verifyDesc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to continue.
mfa.disableDesc: This will remove the extra security from your account. Enter your current authentication code to confirm.
mfa.disableError: Failed to disable 2FA. Check your code.
